currentWave: Session 20 began executing Dylan's 7-decision directive (React/Vite Care Hub UI, TOTP MFA, customer-editable checklists, platform_admin ticket access, legal drafts, Square Sandbox integration, audit-log endpoint) in the specified 10-step order. Steps 1-2 are DONE and live-verified: platform_admin now has every technician ticket capability cross-organization (rbac.js), tickets.js's list-action routing bug is fixed, ticketStore.js/ticketWorkflowStore.js now record real audit events on every write (previously zero audit integration existed for tickets), and a new platform_admin-only audit-log.js endpoint exists with cursor pagination, org/actor/action/date-range filters, and self-auditing access -- 24 endpoints now exist. Steps 3-10 (TOTP MFA, the React/Vite Care Hub itself, Square/email integration, legal drafts, and full accessibility/security/e2e testing) are NOT started -- each is substantial enough to need its own dedicated session(s); see sessions/SESSION_20_RBAC_AND_AUDIT_ENDPOINT.md for the explicit scope decision and what's left.
workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main. Session 17 began under a very large autonomous-continuation directive (17 endpoints, retention/deletion, privacy/consent, remote-support boundaries, F060 AI, F001-F060 workbook regeneration); Session 17 delivered invite-only registration, Session 18 the commercial flow, Session 19 (this one) the remaining 12 endpoints -- completing the endpoint-building portion of that directive. Dylan then asked (this session) for a consolidated list of every remaining decision, to be delivered instead of further silent assumptions, before finishing the rest.

This session (19): 12 more endpoints -- service-records.js (F010), website-profiles.js (F031), entitlements.js (F049), subscriptions.js (F052), technology-assets.js (F043/F041), reminders.js (F048/F037), it-support.js (F044), checklists.js (F046/F047), work-log.js (F025), ticket-workflow.js (F020/F021/F022), activity-timeline.js (F017), metrics.js/templates.js/webhook-events.js (F054/F055/F057). releaseRecommendation: NOT READY. 25 endpoints now exist (audit-log.js viewer plus my-memberships.js) and two real gaps found early this session are now fixed: platform_admin previously could not list or work tickets outside a technician-only 'assigned' check (RBAC bug), and ticketStore.js/ticketWorkflowStore.js had ZERO audit-trail integration despite F008 existing since Session 1 (now every ticket create/transition/triage/prioritize/assign is audited). TOTP MFA for platform_admin is also now built and live-verified (mfa-enroll.js/mfa-verify.js/mfa-manage.js, otpauth + AES-256-GCM secret encryption + hashed recovery codes + a separate short-lived pre-auth cookie) -- MFA_ENCRYPTION_KEY must be set in Netlify before this can be deployed. A React/Vite/TypeScript Care Hub scaffold now exists at care-hub-app/ (builds to /care-hub/, wired into netlify.toml, verified in a real browser) -- which now also has real authentication (login form, MFA enrollment/challenge screens, session-state gating) AND real Tickets/Checklists screens for both customers and staff. Readiness checklists were redesigned from scratch this session for the customer/staff data split (owner decision #3) -- migration 004 applied live, checklistStore.js/checklists.js rewritten, 23-check live smoke test passing, including a real bug found and fixed (stale reviewNote leaking after a checklist was previously returned then re-verified). Step 7 closed the two staff-side UI gaps step 6 left open: a staff checklist review screen (return-for-changes/mark-verified, per-item staff assessment) and a staff cross-org ticket work queue with inline status transitions (reusing work-queue.js/F051 and tickets.js's existing transition action -- no backend changes). Step 9 produced DRAFT-only legal documents grounded in the real schema and dependencies (docs/development/legal/: data-flow/sub-processor inventory, draft Care Hub Privacy Policy naming Resend and Neon as sub-processors, draft Terms of Service with liability/governing-law sections left as explicit attorney placeholders, and a launch-blocking checklist) -- these are NOT attorney-approved and do not by themselves resolve open audit findings F006/F007 against the public site's privacy.html. Step 10 ran a full application security review (RBAC/org-scoping/IDOR across all ~28 endpoints, MFA/auth-flow, SQL injection/XSS/input validation): RBAC/IDOR and SQL/XSS came back clean; fixed a systemic missing-audit-trail gap across 12 store files (scopeOfWorkStore/changeOrderStore/paymentRequestStore/subscriptionStore/assetStore/reminderStore/serviceRecordStore/websiteProfileStore/itSupportStore/workLogStore/approvalStore/entitlementStore/templateStore -- the same class of bug already fixed for tickets in step 1) and a TOTP replay vulnerability (valid codes stayed usable for their full ~90s clock-skew window; now tracked via a per-user last-used-period counter). Step 10 also found one Critical, unresolved finding: mfa-enroll.js had no defense against a password-only account compromise being used to hijack the FIRST TOTP enrollment (attacker with a leaked/phished password, not the device, can enroll their own authenticator before the real owner does). Step 8's email half (chosen over Square, which stays blocked on credentials) PARTIALLY MITIGATES this: mfa-enroll.js's confirm step and mfa-manage.js's disable/reset now send a best-effort security-notification email to the account owner, with delivery outcome always independently audited (mfa.enroll.notification/mfa.disable.notification/mfa.reset.notification, outcome success/failure with a reason) -- this is a DETECTIVE control (owner finds out and can react), NOT a preventive fix (the hijack itself still succeeds regardless of notification delivery); the real fix (out-of-band confirmation required BEFORE enrollment activates) remains the outstanding P0. _lib/email.js's sendEmail() itself was deliberately left best-effort/non-throwing (documented in DECISION_LOG.md why) -- RESEND_API_KEY/EMAIL_FROM (pre-existing in code, newly documented in DEPLOYMENT_PLAN.md this pass) must be set in Netlify for these notifications to actually deliver. Medium/Low findings from step 10 (no server-side length caps on free-text fields, a still-dormant CSV formula-injection gap, a generic error-catch pattern, a narrow rate-limit race, no MFA recovery-code-exhaustion path) are documented with remediation notes in SECURITY_REVIEW.md, not fixed. Full suite: 781/781 passing (up from 763 at the start of steps 9/10/8), npm audit: 0 vulnerabilities in both workspaces. Still missing: QR-code rendering for MFA enrollment, an organization-directory picker for staff (currently manual organizationId entry), a single-ticket-detail route (the work queue doubles as detail), and a live-backend browser smoke test of the full sign-in path (no local Blobs emulation available in this environment). What remains before this could ship: building the real preventive fix for the Critical MFA-enrollment-hijack finding, attorney review/finalization of the Session 20 step 9 legal drafts, the retention/deletion/legal-hold system as enforced code (target periods are now drafted, not yet automated), resolving audit findings F006/F007 against the existing privacy.html, remote-support/automation boundary enforcement, Square Sandbox integration (blocked on Dylan supplying real credentials and checkout-flow input), F060 AI, F001-F060 workbook regeneration, and accessibility/e2e testing -- step 8's Square half is the only item of Dylan's original 10-step directive not yet started.
